name: Grace Mendoza

photo.description: A woman in her late 30s at a pharmacy counter, reading a prescription photo on her phone.

quote: When nobody can read the prescription, it ends up in my hands.

demographics.label: Health Context
demographics.value: Father's hypertension and diabetes; son's asthma

demographics.label: Eligibility Documents
demographics.value: Father's OSCA ID and purchase booklet, as his representative

keyGoals: Turn a prescription photo into a priced, discounted list — no retyping.
One profile per family member, with medicines and refill dates.
See the household's actual monthly savings.
keyBehaviors: Photographs prescriptions before leaving the clinic.
Logs every purchase: what, for whom, at what price.
Checks the senior discount at home, when it's too late to contest.
Combines household refills into one pharmacy trip.

options.value: Monthly, three people in one trip

environment.description: A kitchen counter with a pill organizer, prescriptions, a purchase notebook, and a phone.

painPoints: Deciphering handwriting at the counter under time pressure.
Forgetting whose refill falls due in which week.
No way to know if her father's senior discount was computed right.
opportunities: Photo intake with a confirm screen: verbatim reading beside the interpreted generic.
A savings ledger per member, in pesos.
A printable pharmacy sheet to hand over with the booklet.

name: Miguel Cerveza

photo.description: A young man in his mid 20s checking prices on his phone at night, headset around his neck.

quote: If the generic is the same molecule, why am I paying triple?

demographics.label: Health Context
demographics.value: New hypertension diagnosis via telehealth; first maintenance medicine

keyGoals: Find the generic equivalent and its fair price against the DPRI benchmark.
Proof the generic is the same molecule — registration number included.
Build the refill habit before he breaks it.
keyBehaviors: Telehealth consultations and prescriptions, off night-shift hours.
Price-hunts online at 2 a.m. and screenshots everything.
Hesitates to question counter staff face to face.
Skips or delays purchases when the price feels wrong.

environment.description: A small bedroom desk at night with a phone, a laptop, and an e-prescription PDF on screen.

painPoints: Feeling unable to question a price he suspects is high.
Not knowing which savings are legal entitlements and which are haggling.
opportunities: PDF intake with native text extraction — clean, fast parsing.
Brand-to-generic matches with FDA registration numbers.
The DPRI benchmark as the fair-price anchor.
Refill reminders that survive the night shift — savings without any discount eligibility.

name: Lito Macaraig

photo.description: A retired teacher in his early 70s recomputing a pharmacy receipt with a calculator.

quote: Compute it in front of me. I taught arithmetic for thirty years.

demographics.label: Health Context
demographics.value: Hypertension and diabetes (Losartan 50 mg, Metformin 500 mg)

demographics.label: Eligibility Documents
demographics.value: OSCA ID and purchase booklet, always carried

keyGoals: Know the exact legal counter price before leaving the house.
Catch miscalculations with a figure he can point to.
keyBehaviors: Buys on a fixed date; knows his prices by heart.
Recomputes receipts by hand and questions the difference.
Abandons apps that bury things in menus.
Reads with enlarged text and taps deliberately.

environment.description: A tidy home desk with a calculator, receipts, a purchase booklet, and reading glasses.

painPoints: Feeling he's holding up the line when he questions a price.
opportunities: Step-by-step discount math: VAT off first, then the 20% senior discount.
One savings figure he can state at the counter.
The dose schedule showing 1-0-1 as morning, noon, evening.
Large text and simple flows as the accessibility bar (WCAG).

name: Nica Muñoz

photo.description: A young woman in her late 20s at her laptop, PWD documents on the phone beside her.

quote: The discount is the law, not a favor.

demographics.label: Health Context
demographics.value: Type 1 diabetes; insulin and testing supplies

demographics.label: Primary Intake Door
demographics.value: Program navigator; typed search for purchases

keyGoals: Assert her discount with the law's text and the math in hand.
Find programs that share her insulin cost — and their exact requirements.
Stretch a variable income across a non-negotiable monthly expense.
keyBehaviors: Reads the laws themselves; keeps her PWD documents on her phone.
Knows which pharmacies apply the discount and which push back.
Budgets a fixed medical expense against a variable income.
Asks for the basis of any figure before accepting it.

environment.description: A small apartment workspace with a laptop, an insulin kit, and a folder of program documents.

painPoints: Pharmacies that apply the discount inconsistently or push back.
Program information scattered across offices, posters, and hearsay.
Requirements lists that change without notice.
opportunities: Navigator answers with citations she can show a social worker or pharmacist.
Program matches with document checklists (MAIFIP, Malasakit, PCSO IMAP, Konsulta).
Discount math she can present with her PWD ID.
As-of dates on every answer (MAIFIP was reissued as AO 2026-0031).

name: Andre Valerio

photo.description: A man in his mid 30s at a workstation, pipeline logs and a freshness view on screen.

quote: Every number on screen must trace back to a file, a date, and a version.

demographics.label: Responsibility
demographics.value: Ingestion, data freshness, corpus updates, model configuration

demographics.label: Data In His Care
demographics.value: FDA registry export, DOH DPRI, assistance program corpus

demographics.label: Primary Tools
demographics.value: Admin freshness view, pipeline logs, configuration (GEMINI_MODEL)

demographics.label: Primary Intake Door
demographics.value: None; he operates the pipeline behind the doors

keyGoals: See dataset freshness and failed parses at a glance.
Trace any figure to a source document and ingest version.
Swap the model ID without a code change.
keyBehaviors: Runs ingestion when a source publishes, not on a guess.
Checks failed parses before trusting a fresh dataset.
Re-embeds the corpus when a source changes (e.g., AO 2026-0031).
Keeps the model ID in configuration; the free tier lineup changes.

options.value: Per source release (FDA, DPRI, AO changes)

environment.description: A developer workstation with dashboards for dataset freshness, row counts, and ingestion logs.

painPoints: Scanned government PDFs with dirty text layers.
Silent ingestion failures that surface as wrong answers weeks later.
opportunities: Freshness view: row counts, as-of dates, parse failures.
Provenance and ingest version on every displayed figure.
Config-driven model selection, no deploy required.
